why: The report quotes an alpha-grounded IoU as a robustness check on the headline diagnostic. It was previously asserted without being computed here; this persists it.

alpha_grounded_reference_mask.note: reference side = the stored asset alpha; test side = top-hat re-binarisation

symmetric_rebinarisation.note: both sides through the identical operator; this is what the report table reports

graft_is_not_bit_identical_to_reference.note: The graft is a geometric transform of the reference asset, not a copy of its pixels. Warping and resampling change every value. 'Known by construction' means known by PROVENANCE, not pixel identity.

conclusion: Direction holds under both reference definitions: A ~0.21-0.27, B ~0.62-0.67.
